xauusd.summary: Gold (XAU/USD) trades on the back foot on Tuesday as traders closely monitor developments surrounding the US-Iran negotiations and amid a broad macroeconomic background linked to the prolonged conflict that continues to weigh on the precious metal.

xauusd.title: Silver retreats as firm US Dollar, higher yields weigh on precious metals
xauusd.summary: Silver (XAG/USD) remains under pressure on Tuesday and trades around $76.00 at the time of writing, down 2.13% on the day.

xauusd.title: Gold Price Forecast: Near-term bias remains bearish as 20-day EMA slops downwards
xauusd.summary: Gold price (XAU/USD) trades 0.55% lower at around $4,540 during the European trading session on Tuesday. The yellow metal faces selling pressure as United States (US) Treasury Yields remain broadly firm due to expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) will not cut interest rates this year.

xauusd.title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD sellers retain control whilst below 21-day SMA near $4,650
xauusd.summary: Gold is back in the red around $4,550 in Asia on Tuesday, having faced rejection once again just shy of the $4,600 mark, as markets digest the latest developments around the conflict between the United States (US) and Iran.

xauusd.title: Ghana seeks 30% of large gold mines’ output for local refineries
xauusd.summary: Africa’s largest bullion producer is seeking an additional 10% on top of the current 20% that large mines must sell to the state-owned Ghana Gold Board.
